{"key_event":"Wartime mobilization and welfare-state settlement","reasoning":"Emergency powers, total mobilization, rationing, nationalization, and new welfare institutions pushed an already very capable state to near-maximum domestic penetration, though imperial retreat limited the increase. Civil society also strengthened through mass participation, organized labour, Labour’s electoral mandate, expanded social rights, and more equal voting, despite wartime controls and austerity.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":9.7,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":9.9}
